description_long: This house features a prominent gable roof with a deep overhang, covered in reddish-brown shingles. The front facade is dominated by a large expanse of floor-to-ceiling windows set in a wooden frame, allowing for ample natural light and a modern, open feel. The exterior walls are constructed from brick, providing a sturdy and classic appearance. The side elevations reveal a series of evenly spaced rectangular windows, enhancing the sense of openness and symmetry. The roof extends outward on one side, creating a sheltered area that could serve as a porch or carport. The overall design blends traditional materials with contemporary window arrangements, making it suitable for suburban or semi-urban neighborhoods.
description: A brick house with a large gable roof, expansive front windows, and a modern, open facade.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large triangular gable, floor-to-ceiling windows, and main entryway.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the extended roof overhang and a row of rectangular side windows.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the broad expanse of the roof and the solid brick back wall with minimal openings.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view, again showing the roof extension and side window arrangement.
attribute_window_arrangement: large picture windows on front, symmetrical rows on sides
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway integrated into the glass facade, with a wooden-framed door beneath the gable and no visible porch or steps.
